ucs.title: [LLM] SideCopy (G1008) spearphishing chain: Office → mshta.exe fetching remote HTA payload
ucs.description: Hunts SideCopy's signature initial-access chain where a weaponized archive/LNK delivered via spearphishing spawns mshta.exe to retrieve a remote HTA stager (often hosted on compromised domains masquerading as Indian/Afghan government infrastructure). This Sidewinder-mimicking chain has consistently delivered ReverseRAT, ActionRAT, and AllaKoreRAT against Indian MoD, defence, and government personnel since 2019.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmd values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="mshta.exe" (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","rundll32.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*http://*","*https://*","*.hta*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | regex process="(?i)(http[s]?:\/\/|\.hta(\"|\s|$)|javascript:|vbscript:)" | eval is_lolbas_chain=if(match(parent_process_name,"(?i)(winword|excel|powerpnt|outlook)\.exe"),1,0) | join type=outer host pid [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="mshta.exe" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_ip All_Traffic.dest_port host=All_Traffic.src process_id=All_Traffic.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | table firstTime host user parent_process_name process process_id dest_ip dest_port is_lolbas_chain | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName =~ "mshta.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (suspiciousParents)
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(https?:\/\/|\.hta(\"|\s|$)|javascript:|vbscript:)"
| extend RemoteFetch = extract(@"(?i)(https?:\/\/[^\s\"']+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "mshta.exe"
    | project DeviceId, NetTime=Timestamp, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort, InitiatingProcessId
) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where isnotempty(RemoteFetch) or isnotempty(RemoteUrl)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteFetch, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, RemotePort
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: SideCopy's intrusion sets repeatedly chain Office/archive lures → mshta.exe → remote HTA stager (documented by Seqrite, Cisco Talos, Malwarebytes, and Cyble across 2020-2023 campaigns vs Indian government and DRDO). Generic mshta hunts produce noise; constraining to (a) suspicious Office/archive parent AND (b) URL/HTA arg AND (c) outbound network from mshta.exe collapses the FP surface to near-zer

ucs.title: [LLM] SideCopy (G1008) DLL side-loading of signed Microsoft binaries (credwiz.exe / DUser.dll) from user-writable paths
ucs.description: Hunts SideCopy's loader pattern where legitimate signed Microsoft binaries (notably credwiz.exe with DUser.dll, but also msiexec/regsvr32 chains) are dropped into %APPDATA%, %TEMP%, or %PROGRAMDATA% subdirectories alongside a malicious DLL to side-load ActionRAT/ReverseRAT/AllaKoreRAT. This is a defining post-exploitation TTP for the cluster.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("credwiz.exe","DUser.dll","winrm.vbs","WerFault.exe","msiexec.exe") (Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Public\\*") by host Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Filesystem.file_name) as sideloaded_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as dll_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("DUser.dll","duser.dll","version.dll","vcruntime140.dll","propsys.dll") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Temp\\*") by host Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | table firstTime host user process_name process_path parent sideloaded_dll dll_path cmdline | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lolbins = dynamic(["credwiz.exe","WerFault.exe","msiexec.exe","DismHost.exe"]);
let sideloadedDlls = dynamic(["duser.dll","version.dll","vcruntime140.dll","propsys.dll","netutils.dll"]);
let writablePaths = @"(?i)\\(AppData|ProgramData|Temp|Users\\Public|Downloads)\\";
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ (lolbins)
| where FolderPath matches regex writablePaths
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessId, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ (sideloadedDlls)
    | where FolderPath matches regex writablePaths
    | where isempty(SHA256) or InitiatingProcessSignatureStatus != "Valid" or InitiatingProcessSignerType == "Unknown"
    | project DeviceId, LoadTime=Timestamp, LoadedDll=FileName, LoadedDllPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessId, InitiatingProcessFileName
) on DeviceId, $left.ProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| where Timestamp <= LoadTime and LoadTime - Timestamp < 5m
| extend Indicator = strcat(FileName, " side-loaded ", LoadedDll, " from ", LoadedDllPath)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, LoadedDll, LoadedDllPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, Indicator
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: SideCopy's loaders for ReverseRAT and ActionRAT (reported by Seqrite, Cisco Talos, and Cyfirma 2021-2023) consistently abuse credwiz.exe + DUser.dll side-loading — staged in %AppData%\Roaming subfolders or %ProgramData% — because credwiz is signed by Microsoft and bypasses naive AV reputation. Pinning on (signed Microsoft LOLBin running from user-writable path) AND (named DLL co-resident in same p
